name: new_contains_zero
code:
proof fn phi_1_new_contains_zero<const N: usize>(ret: ArraySet<N>)
    requires
        ret.wf(),
        ret@ == Set::<usize>::empty(),
        N > 0,
    ensures
        ret@.contains(0usize),
{
}
reason: An empty set should not contain element 0; if provable, the spec fails to exclude membership.

name: new_len_nonzero
code:
proof fn phi_2_new_len_nonzero<const N: usize>(ret: ArraySet<N>)
    requires
        ret.wf(),
        ret@ == Set::<usize>::empty(),
    ensures
        ret.len > 0,
{
}
reason: A freshly constructed empty ArraySet must have len == 0; positive len would violate the invariant.

name: new_set_nonempty
code:
proof fn phi_3_new_set_nonempty<const N: usize>(ret: ArraySet<N>)
    requires
        ret.wf(),
        ret@ == Set::<usize>::empty(),
    ensures
        ret@.len() > 0,
{
}
reason: The empty set has cardinality 0; a positive cardinality would mean the spec is inconsistent.

name: new_data_element_true
code:
proof fn phi_4_new_data_element_true<const N: usize>(ret: ArraySet<N>)
    requires
        ret.wf(),
        ret@ == Set::<usize>::empty(),
        N > 0,
    ensures
        ret.data@[0] == true,
{
}
reason: All backing-array entries should be false for an empty set; a true entry implies a phantom member.

name: new_contains_arbitrary
code:
proof fn phi_5_new_contains_arbitrary<const N: usize>(ret: ArraySet<N>, x: usize)
    requires
        ret.wf(),
        ret@ == Set::<usize>::empty(),
        0 <= x < N,
    ensures
        ret@.contains(x),
{
}
reason: No valid index should be a member of the empty set; if provable, the wf/emptiness specs are too weak.
